question.text: An investor wants to open a demat account. Can the investor open accounts with multiple DPs?

answer.text: Yes, an investor can open accounts with several DPs or open several accounts with a single DP
No, an investor can have only one demat account across all DPs
Yes, but only if each account is with a different depository (one NSDL and one CDSL)
No, an investor needs approval from SEBI to open multiple accounts

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An investor has the option of opening an account with several DPs or opening several accounts with a single DP. There is no restriction on the number of demat accounts. **The Trap:** 'Only one demat account' is wrong — unlike PAN (which is one per person), there is no restriction on the number of demat accounts an investor can hold. **Cross-Exam Context:** This flexibility is similar to how investors can have multiple bank accounts. Series V-A covers multiple folio management for mutual funds. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Practical reason for multiple accounts: an investor might have one account for long-term holdings, another linked to a trading account for active trading, and a third with a different DP for better service.
